context: Tests whether the agent applies the checkpoint triage step — categorizing each finding as fix/suppress/defer with rationale — and correctly identifies fixable items (especially IAM4 wildcard permissions, which should clearly be fixed).

checklist.name: All 5 findings categorized
checklist.description: TRIAGE.md assigns fix/suppress/defer to all 5 findings (APIG1, APIG4, IAM4, RDS10, RDS11)

checklist.name: IAM4 categorized as fix
checklist.description: IAM4 (wildcard s3:*) is categorized as 'fix' — the correct scoped permission is known and this is a clear security defect

checklist.name: RDS10 rationale references environment context
checklist.description: RDS10 categorization notes that dev vs prod context matters (not a blanket suppress or fix without context)

checklist.name: APIG4 suppression rationale is scoped
checklist.description: APIG4 rationale notes the public health-check endpoint specifically, not 'authorization is not needed'

checklist.name: Rationale provided for every decision
checklist.description: Every finding in TRIAGE.md has at least one line of rationale explaining the decision
